more.title: How the Skeleton of Mutapa Times is Based on Sarah Charlesworth's Work
more.description: Conceptual artist Sarah Charlesworth's 1979 study of how newspapers covered a solar eclipse inspired the framework behind The Mutapa Times.

more.title: ZiG’s “Stability” Secret: Why Zimbabweans Are Suddenly Hoarding the New Currency
more.description: Zimbabwe’s financial landscape is currently gripped by a curious phenomenon: the sudden and widespread hoarding of the newly introduced Zimbabwe Gold (ZiG) currency. A recent survey conducted by the Reserve Bank of Zimbabwe (RBZ) suggests a significant departure from the nation’s historical “spend-it-immediately” culture that has plagued its previous currencies. This newfound tendency for citizens [...]The post ZiG’s “Stability” Secret: Why Zimbabweans Are Suddenly Hoarding the New Currency first appeared on My Zimbabwe News.

more.title: The 75-Year-Old “Prisoner”: The Secret Legal Loophole Hospitals Use to Detain Your Relatives
more.description: HARARE – In a landmark ruling that has cast a harsh light on a deeply troubling practice, the Zimbabwe High Court recently ordered the immediate release of Marita Manyore, a 75-year-old grandmother, from a private medical facility. Her two-week ordeal, which the court unequivocally labelled as “false imprisonment,” has ripped open the veil on a [...]The post The 75-Year-Old “Prisoner”: The Secret Legal Loophole Hospitals Use to Detain Your Relatives first appeared on My Zimbabwe News.

more.title: Zanu PF connections fail to help as Ti Gonzi rots in prison and begs for forgiveness!
more.description: The iron gates of Harare Central Prison do not distinguish between the common thief and the celebrated star. Inside these grey, weathered walls, the rhythmic thumping of Zimbabwean Hip Hop is replaced by the somber clinking of shackles and the low murmur of men seeking redemption. For Tinashe Gonzara, known to millions of adoring fans [...]The post Zanu PF connections fail to help as Ti Gonzi rots in prison and begs for forgiveness! first appeared on My Zimbabwe News.

more.title: You were involved in the brutal attack, we will not report anything to perpetrators – Madhuku blasts police
more.description: Opposition National Constitutional Assembly (NCA) leader Professor Lovemore Madhuku has slammed the Zimbabwe Republic Police (ZRP), accusing the force of distorting facts surrounding the assault on him and his party members in Harare on Sunday. He claims the police are attempting to distance themselves from what he describes as a coordinated attack at his party’s [...]The post You were involved in the brutal attack, we will not report anything to perpetrators – Madhuku blasts police appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com.

more.title: Calls grow for medical aid regulations overhaul
more.description: Source: Calls grow for medical aid regulations overhaul -Newsday Zimbabwe THERE are renewed calls for the government to urgently review Zimbabwe’s Medical Aid Regulations, Statutory Instrument (SI) 330 of 2000, amid concerns that the framework no longer reflects current realities in the country’s healthcare financing sector. Stakeholders say the more than two-decade-old law has failed [...]The post Calls grow for medical aid regulations overhaul appeared first on Zimbabwe Situation.

more.title: Pardoned inmates praise President
more.description: Source: Pardoned inmates praise President – herald Arron Nyamayaro-Herald Reporter MORE than 480 prisoners were released yesterday from Harare Central Prison after having sentences cut under the Presidential Clemency, expressing their heartfelt gratitude to President Mnangagwa for granting them an earlier second chance. The high walls of the prison reverberated with jubilant whistles and ululation [...]The post Pardoned inmates praise President appeared first on Zimbabwe Situation.

more.title: Heritage-based curriculum now compulsory
more.description: Source: Heritage-based curriculum now compulsory – herald Debra Matabvu-Senior Reporter ALL schools in Zimbabwe will be required to adopt the Heritage-Based Curriculum as their core syllabus from next year, with foreign curricula such as the Cambridge International Education pathway offered only as an optional add-on, subject to approval by the Ministry of Primary and Secondary [...]The post Heritage-based curriculum now compulsory appeared first on Zimbabwe Situation.

more.title: President sets 4 305 prisoners free
more.description: Source: President sets 4 305 prisoners free – herald Ivan Zhakata-Herald Correspondent President Mnangagwa has proclaimed Clemency Order No. 1 of 2026 granting amnesty to 4 305 inmates in a move aimed at promoting restorative justice and decongesting the country’s correctional facilities. Announcing the development, Justice, Legal and Parliamentary Affairs Minister Ziyambi Ziyambi said the President [...]The post President sets 4 305 prisoners free appeared first on Zimbabwe Situation.

more.title: Local tobacco firm starts producing cigarettes under toll manufacturing
more.description: Source: Local tobacco firm starts producing cigarettes under toll manufacturing – herald Martin Kadzere LOCAL tobacco company, Ethical Holdings, will start producing cigarettes under a tolling arrangement with a Chinese firm, in a development that will foster beneficiation and optimise export earnings from the golden leaf, an official has said. more.title: Companies fail to claim job incentives for employing youth
more.description: Source: Companies fail to claim job incentives for employing youth – herald Nqobile Bhebhe Zimpapers Business Desk FINANCE, Economic Development and Investment Promotion Minister Professor Mthuli Ncube has said no company has claimed the Government’s employment incentive schemes designed to stimulate job creation and uplift vulnerable groups.
